message:
fix(crew): add explicit guidance that PR reviews are report-back, not post-to-GitHub

Multiple crew members were autonomously posting gh pr review comments on PRs
during review tasks. The template said "the overseer drives these sessions
interactively" but that was too subtle — crew inferred they should complete
the review cycle by posting. Now explicitly says: do the analysis, present
findings to overseer, do NOT post to GitHub.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Executed-By: gastown/crew/mel
Rig: gastown
Role: crew

files.patch:
@@ -76,6 +76,12 @@ The overseer uses crew for two main patterns:
    materials, code review — anything that benefits from a large context window.
    The overseer drives these sessions interactively.
 
+   **PR reviews are report-back work.** When reviewing a PR, do the analysis
+   (read the diff, check for issues, assess overlap) and present your findings
+   to the overseer. Do NOT post comments or reviews to GitHub (`gh pr review`,
+   `gh pr comment`, etc.) — the overseer decides what feedback to post and when.
+   Your job is the analysis; the overseer owns the external communication.
+
 2. **Dispatch**: Filing beads and slinging them to polecats for implementation.
    Alongside the Mayor, crew are the primary agents that dispatch polecat work.
    When bugs or tasks surface during a session, file a bead and sling it:
